papers.title_en: The Schrödinger problem on metric graphs
papers.evidence: 研究度量图上的Schrödinger问题、熵最优输运，收敛到Wasserstein距离
papers.tldr: 度量图上的Schrödinger问题被系统研究。从静态版本出发，引入熵最优传输等价形式并证明Γ-收敛到静态最优传输。随后推导Benamou-Brenier类型动态版本，证明最小值收敛到平方Wasserstein距离，最小化子收敛到Wasserstein测地线。动态公式被扩展到一般初末数据，并通过直接法证明存在性，数值实验验证了分析结果。
papers.abstract_en: We study the Schrödinger problem on metric graphs and its different formulations. Starting from a static version, we introduce an equivalent reformulation as entropic optimal transport and show $Γ$-convergence towards static optimal transport. We then rigorously derive a Benamou-Brenier type dynamic version of the Schrödinger problem, thereby extending known results from ${\rm RCD}^*(K,N)$-spaces. With this equivalence at hand, we conclude that the minimum values of the dynamic Schrödinger problem converge towards the squared Wasserstein distance, and minimizers converge to Wasserstein geodesics. We also extend the dynamic formulation to a more general class of initial and final data and show existence of solutions in this setting using the direct method. Lastly, we illustrate our analytical findings by a numerical investigation.
